anglophone.title: 2025 was one of most volatile years ever for U.S. naturalizations
anglophone.excerpt: The year began with many people becoming U.S. citizens, but by December, fewer people were doing so, driven by ramped-up scrutiny of applications and eroding trust in the system.

anglophone.title: How a $75 billion windfall from Congress has insulated ICE
anglophone.excerpt: Last year, Congress approved $75 billion for immigration enforcement. That money has allowed ICE to operate nearly unfettered during a record-long shutdown of the Department of Homeland Security.
